script.text: I try to use the terminal key to pry open the envelope. The key scratches the wax seal but doesn't break it. I need something sharper... or just my hands.
I attach the rubber duck to the broken mouse. It squeaks when I click. I've invented the world's worst ergonomic mouse. Patent pending.
I put the duck in the water bottle. It floats! ...I don't know what I expected. This is neither useful nor a bathtub.
I hold the receipt next to the golden token. '1 BTC @ $3.50' vs a golden ZERO token. The irony of crypto in two objects.
I compare the transaction hashes on the printout with the patient records. Wait... some of these wallet addresses match. The patients ARE the traders.
I attach the antenna to the mouse. Congratulations, I've built a wireless mouse. That doesn't work. Because it's broken. And that's not how wireless works.
The floppy disk and the burnt chip. One holds data, the other lost it forever. A cautionary tale about backups.
I type 7314 into the Ledger. 'Invalid PIN.' Of course — the code is for the door, not the wallet. Different kind of security.
